notes: The engineering layer remains scrupulously at the level both Advaita (Shankara) and Vishishtadvaita (Ramanuja) accept: that what survives implementation change is real, that the wise (paṇḍitāḥ) do not grieve what is impermanent or what is permanent. Crucially, the engineering layer does NOT take a position on whether the substrate is non-dual or qualifiedly non-dual — it speaks only of 'the spec' / 'the customer requirement' as the durable invariant, leaving the ontology of that invariant unspecified, exactly as the consensus reading of 2.11 leaves it. Technical Sanskrit terms are preserved with care: paṇḍitāḥ is rendered with its etymological narrowness ('whose knowledge concerns specifically what is invariant under implementation churn'), preserving Shankara's paṇḍā-as-Self-knowledge derivation as a register substitution rather than a flattening. The verse's gatāsūn / agatāsūn pair (the dead / the living) is preserved as 'either side of a deprecation' — bidirectional, as the Sanskrit is. No tradition is named where the engineering layer makes a doctrinal commitment, because the engineering layer makes no commitment that requires tradition-naming. This is the right discipline.
